message:
fix(daemon): correct swap thrash detection inversion, suppress notification oscillation, and add prediction jitter confidence tracking

Three interrelated fixes that eliminate false-positive swap thrash alerts and
notification spam observed on production servers running agent swarms:

1. Swap thrash detection logic inversion fix (cli_app.rs + loop_main.rs):
   The is_swap_thrash_risk() function previously returned true when available
   RAM was HIGH (>= 8GB threshold), which is the opposite of the intended
   behavior. High swap with ample free RAM means cold pages were swapped out
   (normal Linux behavior), not thrashing. Now correctly returns true only
   when available RAM is LOW (< threshold), indicating genuine memory
   exhaustion with active paging. The zram-specific suppression is preserved
   as an additional gate. Tests updated to match corrected semantics.

2. Notification oscillation suppression (loop_main.rs):
   Add cooldown-based notification deduplication to prevent spam from
   pressure level oscillation (e.g., Green→Orange→Green→Yellow→Green→Yellow
   cycling). Within a 5-minute cooldown window, only re-notify if the new
   pressure level exceeds the highest level already notified. Three new
   fields track this state: last_notified_pressure_level,
   last_pressure_notify_time, and last_special_notify (per-location).
   Also fix predictive warning cooldown: stop resetting last_predictive_level
   and last_predictive_warning when predicted_seconds is None, because at
   the red threshold the prediction alternates between Some(tiny) and None
   on consecutive ticks, defeating the 300-second cooldown.

3. Notification threshold alignment (notifications.rs):
   Align PredictiveWarning severity thresholds with check_predictive_warning()
   in loop_main.rs to prevent level-mismatch where the journal shows [CRIT]
   but the cooldown logic considers the event Red. New thresholds: Critical
   < 2 min (was < 5 min), Red < 5 min (was < 10 min), Orange < 30 min
   (unchanged). Fix confidence display to show as percentage (0.92 → "92%"
   instead of "0%"). Add comprehensive tests for confidence caps and
   boundary thresholds.

4. EWMA prediction jitter tracking (ewma.rs):
   Add prediction_jitter_ewma field that tracks normalized prediction
   instability (|Δprediction| / max(prediction, 60s)). Incorporate jitter
   into confidence computation: stability_term = 1/(1 + 3*jitter_ewma).
   New confidence formula: 0.5*sample + 0.2*residual + 0.3*stability
   (was 0.7*sample + 0.3*residual). This penalizes wild prediction swings
   (e.g., 47m → 2m between ticks) that previously produced misleadingly
   high confidence scores.

5. Version bump 0.2.2 → 0.2.5, remove dead test_operator_benchmark module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.2.2"
+version = "0.2.5"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
